key_concepts: [url]Blender 3 | Stylized Rocks the Easy Way | Create & Texture | Full Tutorial^^https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=XvQITUC4vLM[/url]
An easy way to create and texture stylized rocks with just a few simple techniques.

content: Add a cube to the scene. Give it a Subdivision Surface Modifier set to Simple with the Levels at 5 (Image 1). This applies the modifier but does not change the underlying shape, so we can appy the texture to the outside surface. Minimize the Subdivision Surface Modifier and add a Displace Modifier with a new texture renamed to 'Stylized Rock' and a Strength of 0.2 and Midlevel 0.0 (Image 2). Go down to the Texture Properties tab, make sure you are on the Stylized Rock texture and change the Type to Musgrave, the Noise Basis to Voronoi F2-F1 and the Size to 1.0 (Image 3). Go down to the Colors section, check Clamp and Color Ramp, and [k]Ctrl-LMB[/k] the ramp to add a new value and set it to 0.234 (Image 4). Go to the Color Picker and under HSV change the Value to 0.867 and the Alpha to 0.5 (Image 5).

image.caption: Image 1 - Adding a cube to the scene and giving it a Subdivision Surface Modifier set to Simple with the Levels at 5
Image 2 - Minimizing the Subdivision Surface Modifier and adding a Displace Modifier with a new texture renamed to 'Stylized Rock' with a Strength of 0.2 and Midlevel 0.0
Image 3 - Going to the Texture Properties tab and changing the Type to Musgrave, the Noise Basis to Voronoi F2-F1 and the Size to 1.0
Image 4 - Going to the Colors section, checking Clamp and Color Ramp, and adding a new value to the ramp set to 0.234
Image 5 - Going to the Color Picker and changing the Value to 0.867 and the Alpha to 0.5 under HSV

content: You can review the cube now and see it has a rocky surface, but also 12,000+ triangles (Image 6)! We will bring this down by adding a decimate modifier and reducing the Ratio (Image 7). Now add another Decimate Modifier on top of that, set to Planar, 15 degrees (Image 8). Add a Smoot Modifier and set the Repeat to 4 (Image 9). Add a Bevel Modifier, Width Type Percent, Width Percent 10%, Angle 20 degrees, and uncheck Clamp Overlap (Image 10). Add a Weighted Normal Modifier with default settings and a Triangulate Modifier with Keep Normals checked (Image 11).

image.caption: Image 6 - Reviewing the cube and seeing it has a rocky surface but also 12,000+ triangles
Image 7 - Adding a Decimate Modifier and reducing the Ratio to bring the triangle count down
Image 8 - Adding another Decimate Modifier on top, set to Planar at 15 degrees
Image 9 - Adding a Smooth Modifier and setting the Repeat to 4
Image 10 - Adding a Bevel Modifier with Width Type Percent, Width Percent 10%, Angle 20 degrees, and unchecking Clamp Overlap
Image 11 - Adding a Weighted Normal Modifier with default settings and a Triangulate Modifier with Keep Normals checked
